articles.title: Home battery rebates and soaring diesel costs bring electrification to the masses
articles.summary:
What does it take to make a petrol-head tradie home electrification-curious? Try home battery rebates and soaring diesel costs.
The post Home battery rebates and soaring diesel costs bring electrification to the masses appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: A fair energy system is worth fighting for, but without playing the solar and battery blame game
articles.summary:
Claims that solar and battery households are “not paying their way” on the grid – and shifting costs to the have-nots – overstates the problem and misidentifies its cause.
The post A fair energy system is worth fighting for, but without playing the solar and battery blame game appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Arena-backed green hydrogen plan disappears from queue for federal environmental approval
articles.summary:
Arena-backed project hoping to kickstart a major solar and wind-backed green hydrogen hub has been withdrawn from the federal environmental approvals process.
The post Arena-backed green hydrogen plan disappears from queue for federal environmental approval appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Oversize and overmass: State allocates $180m to road upgrades to transport huge wind turbine parts
articles.summary:
State government tips more than $180 million into road network upgrades to support the transport of huge pieces of kit like wind turbine towers and blades.

articles.title: Electric trucks are increasingly competitive, and could be a thing – if only the transport minister would listen
articles.summary:
Updated: It is clear that electric trucks are increasingly competitive. Australia has an opportunity to seize the moment, but is the transport minister listening carefully enough?
The post Electric trucks are increasingly competitive, and could be a thing – if only the transport minister would listen appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Energy Insiders Podcast: Is fuel rationing inevitable?
articles.summary:
How did it come to this? Energy security expert and former deputy chief of the RAAF John Blackburn on how Australia's has done nothing to prepare itself for the current fossil fuel crisis. Plus: EV sales are surging and electric trucks are a thing. Someone tell the transport minister!
The post Energy Insiders Podcast: Is fuel rationing inevitable? appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Pacific Islands energy crisis: One country faces fuel import bills three times its healthcare budget
articles.summary:
Surging oil prices caused by overseas conflict has exposed energy vulnerabilities for Pacific islands and underscored the regional push towards renewables.
The post Pacific Islands energy crisis: One country faces fuel import bills three times its healthcare budget appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: State-owned transmission giant wants to claw back 45 pct more revenue from customers, citing “increased complexity”
articles.summary:
The state-owned network wants to hike its revenue base and add that to customer bills, just as the state government crows about pushing power costs down. 
The post State-owned transmission giant wants to claw back 45 pct more revenue from customers, citing “increased complexity” appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: AGL reaches FID on new peaking plant that will just about pay for itself without ever being switched on
articles.summary:
Australia's biggest coal generator commits to a new peaking power station that will likely recoup its investment in eight years, even if it's not switched on.
The post AGL reaches FID on new peaking plant that will just about pay for itself without ever being switched on appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: ClearVue secures global certification for metal-backed solar panels
articles.summary: Building-integrated PV technology company ClearVue Technologies is a step closer to commercial deployment of its metal-backed solar panels after securing global IEC certification for the integrated rooftop system.

articles.title: Trucking company says fully electric delivery ushers in new era for road freight
articles.summary: Trucking company New Energy Transport says electric road freight is possible now and already commercially viable in Australia after completing what it is calling the country’s first fully electric ‘end-to-end’ heavy road freight delivery.

articles.title: Sungrow launches two energy storage solutions for the Australian market
articles.summary: The PowerTitan 3.0 and Hybrid Solution systems are designed to meet the needs of the rapidly-expanding utility-scale renewable energy sector in Australia.

articles.title: Harmony starts building third 100MW/200MWh French BESS
articles.summary: BESS developer-operator Harmony Energy has started construction on a 100MW/200MWh BESS in France, its third to reach that stage in the country.

articles.title: UL9540A: Shift to system-level testing defines new edition of key BESS safety standard
articles.summary: ESN Premium discusses the sixth edition of the key industry safety standard, UL9540A, with Dana Parmenter of the standards organisation and NRTL, CSA Group.

articles.title: Japan’s first capacity market-contracted BESS brought online by Hexa Energy
articles.summary: Developer-investor Hexa Energy Services has inaugurated what is thought to be Japan’s first operational battery storage project with a capacity market contract.

articles.title: ‘We learned more in the past six months than the past six years’: Australian battery storage lenders navigate merchant risk
articles.summary: Australian BESS face new financing reality as spreads halve to AU$100/MWh and lenders demand 50-70% contracting amid 15GW deployment surge.

articles.title: Grid batteries reach stunning new peak of 44 pct of evening demand in world’s fourth biggest economy
articles.summary:
Battery storage reaches a stunning new record in the world's fourth biggest economy, reaching 44 pct of evening demand. The biggest loser is gas.
The post Grid batteries reach stunning new peak of 44 pct of evening demand in world’s fourth biggest economy appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: UNSW unveils new ageing method to assess TOPCon solar module degradation
articles.summary: UNSW researchers developed a chemically selective, nitrate-based, single-sided accelerated ageing method for TOPCon solar cells that replicates the mildly acidic environment inside EVA-encapsulated modules. The proposed approach enables rapid, physically meaningful screening of front-side metallisation stability, reliably predicting module-level degradation and reducing development time and costs, according to its creators.

articles.title: Tongwei moves into hybrid HJT back-contact solar cell technology
articles.summary: Tongwei is partnering with GS-Solar and Golden Solar to develop a large-scale manufacturing facility for hybrid heterojunction back-contact solar cells that combine HJT passivation, tunneling oxide and polysilicon structures used in TOPCon designs, and the grid-free front-side architecture typical of back-contact technologies.

articles.title: ElectraNet unveils transmission plans to support demand growth
articles.summary: A multibillion-dollar pipeline of electricity transmission upgrades is being proposed by South Australia’s network operator ElectraNet as it seeks to keep pace with “unprecedented” levels of connection demand.

articles.title: RayGen rolls out 1 MW concentrated solar power plant in Brazil
articles.summary: Australian solar and thermal energy storage company RayGen says it has achieved a major international milestone with the commissioning of a 1 MW concentrated solar plant in Brazil.

articles.title: Michigan approves 1,332MW of BESS with 332MW supporting Oracle data centre
articles.summary: The Michigan Public Service Commission has approved six battery energy storage system (BESS) projects totalling 1,332MW of capacity in the US state.

articles.title: 100-hour LDES battery technologies from Form, Noon and Ore: how do they compare?
articles.summary: Form Energy, Noon Energy and Ore Energy are all commercialising proprietary 100-hour battery technologies for LDES applications, but how do they compare on metrics like cost, energy density and round-trip efficiency? We look at what they have revealed, as well as what they haven't.

articles.title: NGEN building 170MWh Austria BESS; Foresight and EBRD invest in Germany, Lithuania developers
articles.summary: BESS platform NGEN has started building an 85M/170MWh BESS in Austria, while Foresight Group and the EBRD have invested in development platforms in Germany and Lithuania.

articles.title: What end-to-end visibility reveals about hidden BESS safety risks
articles.summary: BESS safety issues become all too visible when it's already too late, but a considered approach through the full lifecycle can mitigate these and many other risks, writes Sunny Rai from Intertek.

articles.title: ROUNDUP: Non-lithium energy storage system technology advances in US, Spain, and Scotland
articles.summary: In this news roundup, Unigrid, Inlyte Energy, CIUDEN, and Sunamp are advancing non-lithium energy storage technologies worldwide.

articles.title: Hithium signs 3GWh long-duration BESS agreement with APAC investor Brawn Capital
articles.summary: Hithium has signed a cooperation agreement for a potential 3GWh of battery storage deployments in the Asia-Pacific region with infrastructure investor Brawn Capital.

articles.title: ‘Massive reminder of geopolitical risk’: Australia positioned to capture fleeing battery storage investment amid Middle East tension
articles.summary: Climate Energy Finance’s Tim Buckley argues that geopolitical instability exposes Australia's oil dependency and positions the country as a safe haven for international renewable energy capital.

articles.title: Pushing perovskite solar cells to ultimate thickness limit
articles.summary: Researchers in Singapore have developed fully vacuum-processed ultrathin perovskite solar cells with absorber layers as thin as 10 nm, achieving high transparency and stable efficiencies up to 12%. These cells balance optical transparency and electrical performance, offering scalable, design-flexible photovoltaics suitable for seamless integration into buildings.

articles.title: Rooftop solar boom left apartment residents behind. Here’s how to fix it
articles.summary: Most Australians now understand the basic promise of rooftop solar: lower power bills, cleaner electricity and, for some households, the option to charge an electric vehicle at home for far less than the cost of petrol. But that promise was built around a particular kind of housing – the detached house with a privately controlled roof, a private meter board and a driveway or garage where the owner can install whatever equipment they need.

articles.title: Indonesia’s path to 100 GW of solar
articles.summary: A study from the Institute for Essential Services Reform and Indonesia’s Coordinating Ministry for Economic Affairs explores how Indonesia can turn its target of deploying 100 GW of solar from mandate to mobilisation.

articles.title: Portable data centres to address rising levels of curtailment
articles.summary: Sydney-headquartered start-up WinDC has partnered with United States-based Armada in a move designed to address rising levels of utility-scale solar and wind curtailment in Australia and also satisfy ballooning demand for energy-intensive data centre infrastructure.

articles.title: Transgrid shortlists 2 GW of batteries to strengthen NSW grid
articles.summary: New South Wales transmission company Transgrid has shortlisted nine battery energy storage projects with a combined capacity of 2 GW to provide essential system strength services to the state’s electricity grid from later this year.

articles.title: Chile: Grenergy places 2.6GWh BESS order with BYD, Engie energises 230MWh project
articles.summary: Chile continues to be Latin America’s hotspot for energy storage deployments, with a major new deal for Grenergy and Engie bringing a project online in the past week.

articles.title: European BESS route-to-market optimisers Entrix, GridBeyond raise €55 million
articles.summary: European battery storage optimisers Entrix and GridBeyond, have between them raised €55 million (US$63.22 million) in financing from investors.

articles.title: VIDEO: Grid-forming technology, from niche application to standard requirement
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news proudly presents our webinar with Fluence, 'Grid-forming: From niche application to standard requirement.'

articles.title: 1.6GWh grid-forming Rutherglen Battery receives EPBC Act approval in Australia
articles.summary: A 1.6GWh grid-forming battery energy storage system has received EPBC Act approval in Queensland, Australia.

articles.title: Five battery projects totalling 7.9 GWh advance through approvals
articles.summary: Australia’s massive battery pipeline continues to grow, with projects being filed and making their way through key approvals.

articles.title: Wettest February since 2011 cuts irradiance across most of Australia
articles.summary: As part of its regular update for pv magazine, Solcast, a DNV company, reports that Australia’s wettest February in 15 years produced solar irradiance deficits of 15–30% across the country’s central and eastern regions.

articles.title: Solar sheep grazing delivers margins up to 40% in new study
articles.summary: Agrivoltaic sheep grazing models outperform traditional farming, with higher margins and dual revenue streams from livestock and solar site services, according to a new study out of Canada.

articles.title: Enphase launches AI home energy management system
articles.summary: Enphase Energy has introduced to Australia and New Zealand an artificial intelligence software platform that helps homeowners reduce costs, up clean energy self-consumption and grant control of key home appliances.

articles.title: Sungrow unveils integrated solar storage solution for Australian market
articles.summary: Chinese clean tech manufacturer Sungrow has introduced a hybrid solar energy storage solution that integrates Sungrow's 1+X modular inverter with a dedicated storage interface, PowerTitan 3.0 with built-in DC/DC module, and the solar-ESS Synergy Management System.

articles.title: JinkoSolar achieves 32.76% efficiency in perovskite-TOPCon tandem solar cell
articles.summary: The result was certified by China’s National Photovoltaic Industry Metrology Test Center. The cell utilises a perovskite top device with MBT-ligand-controlled crystallisation on a TOPCon silicon bottom cell, achieving a void-free, high-efficiency tandem structure.

articles.title: Form Energy signs 12GWh agreement to supply multi-day iron-air batteries to new US AI data centres
articles.summary: Form Energy has signed a 12GWh supply agreement in the US for its iron-air batteries with AI data centre infrastructure developer Crusoe.

articles.title: Sungrow bags 1GWh BESS supply deal with ENEVO Group in Romania
articles.summary: Sungrow has signed an agreement with Romanian energy solutions provider ENEVO Group to supply 1GWh of BESS across projects in Romania.

articles.title: Akuo building 80MW/220MWh Portugal BESS for 2027 completion
articles.summary: IPP Akuo will soon start building a 2.75-hour BESS project in Borba, Portugal, integrated into its 181MW Santas solar PV plant.

articles.title: Iberdrola Australia’s 360MWh Broadsound BESS begins commissioning in Queensland
articles.summary: Iberdrola has added its 360MWh Broadsound battery energy storage system (BESS) to AEMO's Market Management System (MMS).

articles.title: Major 1,600 kilometre rail project delivers first solar-powered level crossings
articles.summary: Inland Rail has recently upgraded two level crossings to operate on fully solar-powered systems as part of finishing works on the Narrabri to North Star (N2NS) Phase 1 section of the project.

articles.title: China’s clean tech manufacturing dominance a supply chain risk: report
articles.summary: While the world reels from interruption to crude oil supply chains, a new International Energy Agency report shows clean energy technology manufacturing supply chains are at risk, largely due to the world's reliance on China's advanced manufacturing and distribution expertise.

articles.title: Annual rooftop solar savings total $26 billion: report
articles.summary: The Australian Rooftop Valuation Report, published alongside the official launch of AI-powered platform GreenSketch, finds Australian homes could be saving a collective $26 billion a year on electricity through solar – more than $3,000 annually for an average household.

articles.title: Landowner renewable energy planning platform launched by RELA
articles.summary: RELA has launched an ARENA-backed platform, Assess 2.0, which gives landowners upfront insight into suitability for their property to host renewable energy projects, prior to entering negotiations with developers.

articles.title: esVolta secures US$140 million financing for 300MWh Texas battery storage project
articles.summary: esVolta has closed a US$139.6 million project financing package with MUFG Bank for its 300MWh Boxcar energy storage project in Texas, US.

articles.title: ‘Do not overlook it at pre-construction’: Gore Street Capital’s head of asset management on BESS data
articles.summary: BESS fund manager Gore Street Capital's director of asset management Daniel Sherlock-Burke recently discussed the work it is doing around capturing and making use of its huge quantities of operational data.

articles.title: Bluetti owner PowerOak files for Hong Kong IPO: the fourth major player in global portable energy storage
articles.summary: Shenzhen PowerOak New Energy Co., Ltd. (PowerOak), the fourth key player in the global portable energy storage sector, has submitted its IPO application to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

articles.title: ‘Absolutely bright’ future for battery storage in Ukraine
articles.summary: Ukraine's need for energy storage presents opportunities for brave investors, Energy-Storage.news hears from Vadym Utkin, energy storage lead at DTEK.

articles.title: Australia’s Transgrid shortlists 2GW of grid battery storage to replace coal’s system strength in NSW
articles.summary: Transgrid has shortlisted nine utility-scale battery storage projects, totalling up to 2GW, to provide critical system strength services.

articles.title: New Zealand port demonstrates battery storage potential amid complex tariff landscape
articles.summary: New Zealand's fragmented electricity market structure is creating both opportunities and obstacles for industrial battery storage, as demonstrated by CentrePort Wellington's upcoming deployment.

articles.title: Ultra-low-cost solar research models 2,000 GW-scale solar market potential
articles.summary: Researchers from two Australian universities have demonstrated that ultra-low-cost solar could deliver to national energy systems 1,000 TWh per year for domestic use and 2,600 TWh per year for export.

articles.title: Sekisui-led consortium testing film-type perovskite solar for agrivoltaics
articles.summary: A Japanese consortium is piloting agrivoltaics using film-type perovskite solar cells over rice paddies to study energy generation alongside crop production. The three-year project will assess impacts on rice yield, land use, and emissions while testing the technology’s performance and commercial viability.

articles.title: CSIRO says robots shape as game-changer for solar farm maintenance
articles.summary: Australia’s national science agency says AI-driven robots could reshape solar maintenance across large-scale PV projects after successfully trialing the cutting-edge technology.

articles.title: American Battery Factory secures 4.5GWh offtake agreements for Arizona LFP gigafactory
articles.summary: American Battery Factory (AFB) has secured offtake agreements for 4.5GWh of its initial 5.5GWh US factory output.

articles.title: VIDEO: BESS technology trends with Marek Kubik
articles.summary: Ex-Fluence alumnus Marek Kubik sat down with Energy-Storage.news at last month's Energy Storage Summit 2026 in London to talk big picture BESS technology trends, including energy density, LDES and sodium-ion.

articles.title: ‘It’s all about oil, isn’t it?’: BESS and EV expansion in the US vs Europe
articles.summary: As battery energy storage systems (BESS) and electric vehicle (EV) infrastructure expand in the US and Europe, a clear contrast is becoming evident in their market growth.

articles.title: Belgium: IPP Storm raises €330 million, starts building 1.2GWh battery storage projects
articles.summary: Renewables developer and IPP Storm has secured project finance for two large-scale BESS projects in Ruien and Langerlo, Belgium.

articles.title: US will install 500GWh of new energy storage by 2031, Wood Mackenzie predicts
articles.summary: Wood Mackenzie has forecast that the US energy storage industry will install around half a terawatt-hour of new capacity over the next five years.

articles.title: Storage-firmed renewables: Rio Tinto and Australian governments commit AU$2 billion to future-proof Boyne smelter
articles.summary: AU$2bn deal secures Boyne smelter to 2040+ with 600MW+ battery storage firming renewables for Rio Tinto's aluminium production in Queensland.

articles.title: Ausgrid’s 400MWh Steel River East BESS clears NSW planning hurdle despite community opposition
articles.summary: The New South Wales IPC has approved Ausgrid's 400MWh Steel River East BESS located within the Hunter and Central Coast REZ.

articles.title: Arevon starts building 1GWh California BESS; BPP and Energy Vault buy Texas projects
articles.summary: Arevon, Banpu Power (BPP) and Energy Vault have made BESS project announcements totalling 1,550MWh of capacity in the US.

articles.title: Nebraska advances energy storage and data centre-focused bill
articles.summary: The legislative body of Nebraska, US, has advanced a bill to clarify and expand the state’s use of battery energy storage systems (BESS).

articles.title: Energy Storage Awards 2026 opens for entries: It’s time to shine and show the world
articles.summary: The industry’s premier celebration of excellence, innovation and hard work returns this autumn, at the Energy Storage Awards 2026.

articles.title: ERCOT after RTC+B: How real-time optimisation is reshaping battery storage economics
articles.summary: Tapas Peshin of energy management software provider PCI Energy Solutions, on one of the biggest changes in Texas' ERCOT market for battery storage assets.

articles.title: VIDEO: Grid-forming, LDES and solar hybrids in Europe, with Envision Energy’s Michael Koller
articles.summary: Envision Energy's director of energy storage solutions, Michael Koller, speaks with Energy-Storage.news at Energy Storage Summit 2026 in London, UK.

articles.title: Munch Energie connects 500MWh German BESS cluster to the grid
articles.summary: Utility and power firm Munch Energie is putting five large-scale BESS projects online in Germany.

articles.title: Australia’s EPBC Act pipeline swells with 5.5GWh of battery storage across three states
articles.summary: 5.5GWh of battery energy storage projects have been added to Australia’s Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act.

articles.title: ‘Culture of collaboration needed’: Australia’s NSW pushes energy storage projects from ambition to operation
articles.summary: Thimo Mueller, general manager of commercial at ASL, has urged that a ‘culture of collaboration’ will be key to New South Wales (NSW) achieving its energy storage targets.

articles.title: Elevate Renewables developing 600MWh New Jersey BESS through state’s energy storage programme
articles.summary: The New Jersey Board of Public Utilities (NJBPU) has selected developer and IPP Elevate Renewables’ 600MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) for development in Ridgefield, New Jersey, US.

articles.title: Estonian state-owned firm Eesti Energia to add BESS totalling 184MWh to Lithuania wind farms
articles.summary: Enefit, the electricity business of state-owned Estonian utility and power generator Eesti Energia AS, will build three new battery energy storage systems (BESS) in Lithuania, with a combined capacity of 46MW/184MWh.

articles.title: Exclusive interview: Argentina’s Energy Secretary on BESS tender and new era for country’s energy sector
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news spoke exclusively with Argentina's National Energy Secretary, María Tettamanti, about the launch of its second BESS tender.

articles.title: India: ACME Solar commissions 480MWh of BESS, readies for FDRE and peak supply deals
articles.summary: ACME Solar has commissioned just over 480MWh of battery storage in Rajasthan, India, following the recent signing of several storage-backed renewable energy deals.

articles.title: Australia: 1.6GWh Belah BESS submitted for approval as 590MWh Mornington project enters AEMO commissioning
articles.summary: Eku Energy and Valent Energy have recorded separate milestones in their respective expansions of battery energy storage systems (BESS) in Australia.

articles.title: AGL commissioning ‘ambitious’ 1,000MWh Liddell grid-forming BESS in Australia
articles.summary: Australian utility AGL has started commissioning its 500MW/1,000MWh Liddell battery energy storage system (BESS) in New South Wales.

articles.title: Quinbrook bags EPBC Act approval for 780MW Supernode North BESS in Australia
articles.summary: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners has received environmental clearance for its Supernode North battery energy storage system (BESS) project in Queensland, Australia.

articles.title: ‘US battery manufacturing will surpass 100% of demand’, US ESC report states
articles.summary: A new report from the US Energy Storage Coalition (ESC) indicates that the American battery manufacturing sector surpasses 100% of US demand for energy storage systems (ESS) and modules.

articles.title: Energy Vault continues to diversify as it experiences Q4, full year 2025 growth
articles.summary: Energy storage developer and system integrator Energy Vault has released its Q4 and full year 2025 financial results, showing growth credited to entering the AI infrastructure market, expanding projects in Australia and its ‘Asset Vault’ subsidiary.

articles.title: VIDEO: Financial strategies for energy storage assets, beyond arbitrage
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news proudly presents our webinar with GridBeyond, 'Beyond Arbitrage: Financial Strategies for Energy Storage Assets.'

articles.summary:
Batteries are delivering higher peak morning supply than gas. Gas is still twice as large as batteries in the evening but it's losing share.

articles.title: Why an oil crisis is bad news for Australia’s biggest coal state – and how to break the cycle
articles.summary:
One state in Australia remains particularly vulnerable to global oil shocks because it hasn't built the replacement capacity for coal. Batteries will help – and much more wind.

articles.summary:

articles.title: Powerful new rooftop solar panel promises system sizes “previously out of reach”
articles.summary:

articles.summary:
An in-depth webinar exploring the next phase of residential battery storage in Australia, brought to you by GoodWe.

articles.title: A battery with every turbine: Huge wind project seeks federal green tick with novel storage plan
articles.summary:

articles.title: Remote Samoan island communities to receive Australian-backed rooftop solar
articles.summary: Remote Samoan island communities will receive roof top solar, battery storage and a mini-grid upgrade to cut dependence on diesel, kerosene, wood fires and disposable batteries for light and cooking, with the Australian government's REnew Pacific assistance.

articles.title: First Nations clean energy strategy implementation funds now available
articles.summary: Australian government grants are now available for First Nations communities, organisations and groups for advice and engagement on planning, negotiating or pursuing clean energy projects in homes, communities and on Country.

articles.title: VicGrid tenders for three Latrobe Valley-based synchronous condensors
articles.summary: VicGrid is inviting expressions of interest from developers to partner in the delivery of at least three new synchronous condensers at or near the Hazelwood terminal station in the Latrobe Valley.

articles.title: Edify Energy Burroway solar farm and BESS lands NSW green light
articles.summary: Edify Energy's proposed Burroway 100 MW solar farm and 400 MWh battery energy storage system in the Central-West Orana Renewable Energy Zone has been approved by the NSW government, subject to development consent conditions.

articles.title: New York leads US in BESS moratoriums, recent data shows
articles.summary: New York, US is leading the country in battery energy storage system (BESS) moratoriums, with 97 moratoriums in place, according to renewables service provider Carina Energy.
